task_objective: Land the lunar module softly, upright, and centered between the flags, with minimal fuel usage, avoiding crashes and bouncing.

phase_structure.purpose: encourage measurable movement toward the landing pad center and descent toward the surface
phase_structure.reward_intent: negative potential that shrinks as the lander gets closer to (x=0, y=0); cannot be maximized without reaching the target

phase_structure.purpose: encourage upright orientation, low lateral and angular velocities throughout, and gentle vertical speed near the ground
phase_structure.reward_intent: small penalties for deviations from upright, high lateral/angular speeds, and an additional vertical-speed penalty when altitude is low

phase_structure.purpose: reward a soft, stable landing and penalize hard impacts
phase_structure.reward_intent: large positive reward when both legs are down with very low speeds and upright angle; large negative reward if both legs touch at high speed

component_blueprint.name: r_progress_guidance
component_blueprint.design_intent: provide a continuous progress signal that increases (less negative) as the lander moves toward the target, without rewarding passive loitering
component_blueprint.anti_exploit_note: max value (-0) is only obtained exactly at center on the ground; any stationary high-altitude position far away yields a large negative reward

component_blueprint.name: r_stability_quality
component_blueprint.design_intent: suppress wobble, tilt, and lateral drift throughout the flight to promote smooth, stable behavior
component_blueprint.anti_exploit_note: penalties are small and apply everywhere; an agent cannot gain reward indefinitely by ignoring progress because the progress penalty dominates, and the stability penalty is always non-positive

component_blueprint.name: r_soft_landing_vertical
component_blueprint.design_intent: discourage high vertical speed when close to the ground, encouraging a gentle touchdown
component_blueprint.anti_exploit_note: only active when y < 0.5; cannot be exploited for infinite reward because it is a penalty that becomes zero only when vy is zero near ground

component_blueprint.name: r_control_cost
component_blueprint.design_intent: penalize engine effort magnitude to promote fuel efficiency
component_blueprint.anti_exploit_note: uses absolute values of both engine commands, so no sign can accidentally yield positive reward

anti_exploit_principles: Progress shaping uses a distance potential that reaches its maximum only at the landing spot; no passive hovering can maximize it.
Stability/alignment penalties are purely non-positive and additive; they support the progress signal rather than substituting it.
Action penalties use absolute values to avoid rewarding one direction.
Terminal success is gated on primitive contact and low-speed conditions, preventing credit for bouncing or slamming.
